DI_dcTYRhXy: I'm stoked to share my final project for Creative Technology class - a fully functional RC car built from the ground up! I’m taking my students through every step of the design and build process. #CreativeTech #STEMEducation #MakerMovement #RCBuild
DLEEVBEprcY: This week I’m leading Creative Tech at Artios Outpost, teaching 10-12 year olds how to 3D model. We started with Fusion360 and then jumped into ZBrush sculpting. It’s been awesome watching them create and pick up new skills so fast.
C8uXYXYAsz6: 🌟 Junior High Project Showcase! 🌟 We are excited to share the final project from our Junior High 3D Design and 3D Printing II class: Arduino Controlled Cars! 🚗💡 Guided by Mr. Daws, our students designed, 3D-printed, and programmed their own remote-controlled cars using Arduino, featuring IR sensors, proximity sensors, and motors. This project combined electronics, coding, and design, giving students hands-on experience and practical skills. Check out the video to see their hard work in action! 🎥✨ #STEMEducation #ArduinoProjects #FutureEngineers #HandsOnLearning

Cp3fY4ruDQS: I made these Go/No-go gauges on my @tormach Cnc mill. Client was a networking company. The gauges are meant to easily tell the difference between different sizes of BNC connectors.  That was a fun little project. #madewithtormach #tormach #cncmachining
CoWM1mxODCW: A client asked me to make a hotend adapter plate for a 3D printer. It’s so tiny!  This was such a fun challenge.
CoYdsRurxFZ: Made this prototype for a client in my CNC lathe!  Very satisfying to put it all together. #madewithtormach
CoFjTNbNf1I: Knurling on a CNC machine!  Took some research to wrap my head around it but now I got it figured out!  #madewithtormach
CnmOi7-PIWR: I was asked to fix a part on a golf cart that had been driven into a tree. I cut off the old bent part, machined a replacement, and welded it onto the original. A quick coat of paint and good as new. Let me know if I can fix anything for you! (Link in Bio to learn more) #recycle #welding #machining
CnHbyDoJiv8: This new fidget toy is now on my #etsy shop: FidgetCraft: a Magnetic Fidget Slider and Building Toy. @fidgetcrafttoys This was super fun to develop and test out on my siblings and nieces and nephews. Plus I’ve had loads of fun playing with it. It’s so satisfying!  Check it out over on my store. Link in bio!  #maker #edc #fidget #fidgettoys #satisfying #satisfyingvideos #haptic #asmr
CnWyev2p57w: FidgetCraft: a Magnetic Fidget Slider and Building Toy. Check it out over on my #Etsy store. Link in bio! @fidgetcrafttoys #maker #edc #fidget #fidgettoys #satisfying #satisfyingvideos #haptic #asmr #3dprinting
CnsUsSEpsdb: Working on the injection mold for my @fidgetcrafttoys - once I get the mold working, I’ll be able to crank out these toys faster and they’ll look like real things and not just 3D printed. #fidgettoys #injectionmolding #cncmachining
Cn8e-yyJzIE:
I created magnetic shoes for Lego Minifigs that work with @fidgetcrafttoys!  Oh my goodness!  Game changer.

www.FidgetCraft.com
Cn3LBKwjvAG: I gave my big brother a set of FidgetCraft tonight (the DICE and DOMINO) and he discovered this amazing trick you can do with it. My nephews and I lost it! So cool. I don’t understand it but I love it! #fidget #fidgettoys #fidgettoy #fidgetslider #stemtoys #stemtoysforkids
CehP5nFr23I: Today I shipped off my first Etsy order! I’m enjoying dipping my toe into the product world. Here’s a fun little reel showing a little bit of the process of how I make my magnetic action figure blister packs. (Etsy store link in bio) #actionfigures #toys #makersgonnamake #vacuumforming
Ch7jk4frlEm: I had the immense pleasure of fabricating this steadi-cam sled dolly for a production shooting in the Atlanta area. On top of that, I was able to visit the set to deliver it to the grip team working on the show. After I dropped it off, I got a text from the best boy telling me that the dolly was already used to film a shot!  Wow. How Marvelous! 😉🤫 I always wanted to make movies. I never dreamed I’d make gear that would be used to make movies. Life can be weird sometimes.  #maker #fabricator #productionlife #grip #gear #setlife
CkTzPfGuGKN: I was given this part to make 3 copies. It attaches to a film dolly. I modeled it in Fusion360 and made it in my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill. Really excited about how these turned out. Just gotta deburr and press in the pin and then I’ll take em to set and drop them off to the client. #gripgear #filmgear #movieset #cncmachining #madewithtormach
CkJjljmO8Jb: First parts made on my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill.  These are for a grip working in the film biz here in Atlanta. #cncmachining #gripgear #filmgear
CjosDmZOj2x: First project on my @tormach 1100MX CNC mill — Gussets for this 90 degree camera plate. Simple project but it made this infinitely faster than having to spot drill, drill, and tap 8 holes on the manual mill. Feeling more familiar with this amazing machine. #cncmachining #madewithtormach
Cl91z12sBgl: Someone asked me to make a replacement part for a German Mauser bayonet from WWII. I love having the right tools for the job!  I’m definitely putting my @tormach machines to good use. Check out my process! #cnc #cncmachining #cncmachinist #madewithtormach
CgQU46wDB53: Congrats to @lanier_tech student @jeremiahdaws for bringing home the SILVER medal at this year’s national @skillsusa competition in the category CNC Turning Specialist. #skillsusa #machinist
Cl1LeEyvyk-: Lathe II students working on their final project for the class - a crankshaft! They have learned so much over their time in this program.  #machining #machinist #trade #machinistlife #instamachinist
CW8h1F7gjQM: Student offset turning a crankshaft this morning.  #machining #machinist #machineshop @lanier_tech
CeUGILPpdco: Heat treat students pulling their 1-2-3 blocks out of the oven. Air cooling. That glow is so pretty!  #machinist #machining
Clge2bvOj26: Mill I student using a radius cutter. #whysoserious #manualmachinist #machinist
CkGYtR_Lzj-: What is a machinist’s favorite Halloween costume?  #machinist #machining #halloweencostume #machineshop
CXg057KpGk0:
‘Twas the weeks before Christmas and all through the shop, not a lathe chuck was spinning… 

Machine tool lab is empty and quiet without the students here to run the machines. #machineshop #laniertech @lanier_tech #machining #machinist #instamachinist
CZMl5sZrDej: Big day in the shop!  Intro Students learning how to tram a mill head. These are necessary first steps in their machining journey. #machining #machinist #instamachinist @lanier_tech
Cacr9InFgSQ: Congrats to our students who took Gold in CNC Turning, Silver in CNC Milling, and Silver in CNC Technician at the Skills GA competition!  Great work! And kudos to Instructor/Sensei Tommy Morris!  @lanier_tech @skillsusagaps #cncmachining #cncmachinist #skillsusa #skillsusageorgia

CdBLhhRPHVb: Sometimes inspiration hits you late at night. Last night around midnight I decided to try and make a vacuum forming machine. So using a laser to cut out the box and an aluminum welder to make the frame, I was able to do a couple of successful vacuum pulls. This can be used to make molds or to create packaging for a future product. I am really excited about possibilities. I still have a lot to learn but this is a great first step.  #vacuumforming #maker
CeJ-u9tlqXS: I made this lightsaber for a friend who wanted to cosplay Mitaka from Star Wars Visions for @starwarscelebration this weekend. I couldn’t attend this year but glad to be a small part of it.  Looks like everyone is having a blast. (Made on my CNC lathe @tormach, and my manual mill and lathe.). #starwarscelebration #lightsaber #machining #madewithtormach #machinist #cncmachining
CeU9MgPLKjF:
My latest creation - a magnetic action figure display pack.  Display your favorite action figures ANYWHERE!  I’m super excited about this - I’ve launched it for SALE on my Etsy store! (Link in BIO)

These magnetic action figure blister packs are perfect for the action figure collector who likes to open their toys but also display them.  The blister pack evokes the pristine nature of a action figures and the unique use of magnets allows you to display your action figures on refrigerators, filing cabinets, dry erase boards, etc...

These are the perfect size for Star Wars, G.I. Joe, or whatever 3 3/4 inch action figures you want to show off.
 
#actionfigures #toys #nostalgia #80stoys #display #custom #displaycase #blisterpackaging #vacuumforming #maker #etsy #etsyshop
DP6sbsxEW32: Playing with Butane rockets. Mix butane and water and flip = rocket!  #science #rocket #fununcle
DKODiRspLkJ:
Finally put up the zipline from our pirate ship treehouse. Been meaning to do it for a while, and the boys took the lead and made it happen.

Everyone in the family had to try it. Some were braver than others. A few questionable landings. Lots of laughs.

I love this crew. We’re always up to something fun, and I wouldn’t trade it for anything.
#Zipline #PirateShipTreehouse #MemorialDayFun #familytime
CkgNYQ7u7Lm:
This was a surprisingly fun project. I was asked to fix a trailer. The tongue had been bent pretty badly when it was jackknifed.  I cut off the bent portion and welded a new section on and reinforced the joint on the inside of the c channel. Then I relocated the spare tire assembly.  I love how it turned out!

I’ve said it before - everyone should learn how to weld. It’s such a handy thing to know.  Plus it’ll be useful in the coming zombie apocalypse.  Still waiting on someone to ask me to put spikes on their vehicle. One day. 😜 #welding #maker #diy #weldlife
CurdmwYOsHR: Here’s another prop I made for a high school production of The Little Mermaid. King Triton’s staff. I 3D modeled it using a still from the animated movie so the profile is just about screen accurate.  Very happy with the look but huge lesson learned on this one - don’t 3D print stage props. This thing broke a lot and I ended up having to fix it with epoxy glue and clay multiple times - sometimes even during the performances!  That was stressful but fun to experience the excitement of live theater in some small way. #propmaking #stageprops #littlemermaid
CUTDMc2JOOM: Finished my Mando helmet project. Now all my nephews have one. Took longer than I wanted it to but it’s been a crazy year. Started this project back in December.  Death in the family, change of career, starting school, 3D printer failed a few times, etc… Feels good to have closure on this project. Sadly, now I kinda feel like the boys need a full suit of Mando armor. 🤦🏻‍♂️ Haha. #mandolorian #cosplay #maker #3dprinting
BpLiah6g-bP: I’ve been working on this project for several months.  My favorite science fiction weapon of all time - The Pulse Rifle from Aliens. I 3D printed the various pieces, glued it together, filled the seams, filled and sanded the print lines, painted it (and repainted the green because the first color was wrong), weathered it, and added the faux digital read out complete with Easter egg number. I can’t tell you how satisfying it is to hold this thing in my hands. #ripley #aliens #pulserifle #maker #3dprinter #eastereggismybirthday
CfWM5WpupZ_: Last week I competed in the national @skillsusa competition in Atlanta. It’s a competition for students in trade school. My category was CNC Turning Specialist. I had to program a part from a blueprint and take some tests. I ended up placing 2nd in the nation. I got a silver medal and $3000!  There was a whole awards ceremony at State Farm Arena (see video). With my time in trade school coming to an end in a few weeks, this was an amazing finale to the whole experience. #cncmachining #tradeschool

Step into the spotlight with us as we unveil a captivating journey behind the scenes of Artios Academies of Sugar Hill’s latest theatrical production—The Little Mermaid. 🎥This feature-length documentary is your exclusive pass to witness the passion, dedication, and camaraderie that transform long days and late nights into an enchanting spectacle. ⭐️ From heartfelt moments to delightful shenanigans, experience the pulse of creativity, collaboration, and genuine connection as we showcase why this event is the pinnacle of the Artios experience.

A suggested donation of $10 per 🎟️ will contribute to enhancing our Film Department and allow us to obtain cutting-edge equipment like cinema lenses. These lenses are essential for producing short films, documentaries, promotional pieces, and enhancing the filming quality for upcoming shows.
